text:
Market today: Tech is bouncing back after a global AI/chip selloff rattled the sector. The rebound is real but fragile - you're in a margin account, so a fake recovery that rolls over hits you twice as hard.

Your holdings: AVGO (your largest, down 12.89%) is the biggest risk - it took the chip selloff on the chin and a continued slide toward $336 stop means real damage amplified by margin. PLTR (down 10.31%) is bleeding with no clear floor yet, watch that $85 stop closely. CRWD (down 10.29%) is in the same boat - three names each down ~10% on margin is a serious combined drag. SPCX is a brand-new, extremely volatile IPO with thin data - treat it as a live grenade, manage size manually.

Watch: Whether today's tech rebound has legs or fades into close - a weak close would be a red flag. Also watch chip-sector reaction to SK Hynix's $29B Nasdaq listing news, which adds supply pressure.

Bottom line: Three margin positions each down ~10% is your real problem - don't let a bounce talk you out of managing AVGO and PLTR size.
